description: Rogers-O'Brien lunch prep sheet assumed a sales/pursuit framing without asking David for meeting context. Defaulted to 'account pursuit' and 'conversation strategy' language when the meeting was actually a friendly peer-level show-and-tell. Also didn't ask about existing relationships before researching attendees cold.
correction: For any externally-organized meeting prep, ask David for the meeting's purpose and relationship context before building the sheet. Don't default to a sales framing for meetings David personally organized with known contacts.
systemic_fix: When building prep sheets for meetings David organized with external attendees: (1) ask for meeting purpose/context first, (2) assume warm relationships exist, (3) don't default to sales framing unless David says it's a sales meeting.
